catalog.title: Listen & read · Conversation routines you can reuse
catalog.description: Conversation routines you can reuse · A2 listen & read in “Culture & integration context”: Dutch you can use the same week, guided steps, and a short check at the end.

pedagogy.objective: By the end of this lesson, you can understand short input about culture & integration context, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions
can_do_outcomes: Understand basics of primary school routines and parent contact (rough picture)
Describe one or two civic habits (cycling, calendars, punctuality) simply
Follow a short exchange in this theme and reuse one chunk.

examples_nl: In Nederland dragen sommige mensen oranje.
Mag ik vragen hoe jullie Koningsdag vieren?
examples_en: In the Netherlands some people wear orange.
May I ask how you celebrate King's Day?

examples_nl: Men plant sociale afspraken vaak een dag van tevoren.
Je spreekt hier vaak rustig aan bij de balie.
examples_en: People often plan social meetups a day ahead.
Here people often address the desk calmly.

grammar_points.point: fixed expressions for holidays and greetings
examples_nl: Integratie is taal én gewoontes leren.
Fijne feestdagen en tot volgend jaar!
examples_en: Integration is language and learning habits.
Happy holidays and see you next year!

micro_outcomes: I can recognise key words about culture & integration context in short Dutch texts or audio.
I can copy at least one useful chunk into a sentence about myself.
I can finish most guided tasks in the lesson without peeking at translations on the second try.
I can pass the quick self-check at the end of the lesson.
I know what to revise tomorrow in two minutes or less.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Understand short input about culture & integration context, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions

**Grammar thread today:** General statements (men / je / veel mensen …) — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Culture & integration context**.

Words you might use today:
• **borrel**
• **traditie**
• **integratie**
• **Koningsdag**
• **Sinterklaas**
• **kerst**
• **oud en nieuw**
• **basisschool**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.example_response: Gisteren sprak ik met een vriend over **Koningsdag** en Nederlandse tradities. We dronken koffie en wandelden een half uur door de buurt.

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Trainer:** Nederlanders plannen graag. Een appje van tevoren is normaal.

**Deelnemer:** En als ik iets last-minute afzeg?

**Trainer:** Geef het zo snel mogelijk door. 'Sorry, het lukt niet' is oké.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.

**Today's grammar — general statements**

**Rule:** *In Nederland …* · *Veel mensen …* · *Je …* (one) — useful for culture talk without blaming.

**Patterns:**
• *In Nederland fietsen veel mensen.* · *Je spreekt hier rustig aan bij de balie.*

**Produce:**
1. One neutral sentence about a habit with *In Nederland …*.
2. One careful question: *Mag ik vragen of …?*

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — ui / eu / ij:** round lips for *huis — leeuw*; *mijn* is one smooth glide.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**impersonal constructions (men …, je … as 'one')**
• NL: In Nederland dragen sommige mensen oranje. / Mag ik vragen hoe jullie Koningsdag vieren?
• EN: In the Netherlands some people wear orange. / May I ask how you celebrate King's Day?

**present tense narratives about customs**
• NL: Men plant sociale afspraken vaak een dag van tevoren. / Je spreekt hier vaak rustig aan bij de balie.
• EN: People often plan social meetups a day ahead. / Here people often address the desk calmly.

**fixed expressions for holidays and greetings**
• NL: Integratie is taal én gewoontes leren. / Fijne feestdagen en tot volgend jaar!
• EN: Integration is language and learning habits. / Happy holidays and see you next year!

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.


**Culture tip:** This unit foregrounds situated cultural knowledge; every lesson links language to real Netherlands contexts.
*How does this compare with where you live? One sentence in your own words.*

**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **borrel** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **traditie** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **integratie**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **borrel** or **traditie**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"In Nederland dragen sommige mensen oranje." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Trainer: Nederlanders plannen graag. Een appje van tevoren is normaal.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Trainer: Nederlanders plannen graag. Een appje van tevoren is normaal.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **borrel** and fits *General statements (men / je / veel mensen …)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Phrase bank (use freely)**
• borrel
• traditie
• integratie
• Koningsdag
• Sinterklaas
• kerst
• oud en nieuw
• basisschool
• ouderspreekuur
• directheid
• fietsen
• agenda

**Your task:** Write or say **4–6 short sentences** in Dutch about this unit theme.
Rules: include **at least one question** and one connector (**en**, **maar**, or **want**).

**Example starter (you can change it):**
Vandaag … Ik wil … **want** … **En jij?**

**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **borrel**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag borrel met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

You reached the end of this A2 path — huge win. Mix review, scenarios, and real-life practice from here.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

Integration courses stress both **language** and **local habits**: waste rules, GP registration, and school communication. Asking *Wat is hier normaal?* shows respect and buys patience.

**Phrases — school & integration tone**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Mag ik een afspraak met de leerkracht?* | May I have an appointment with the teacher? |
| *Mijn kind heeft moeite met huiswerk.* | My child struggles with homework. |
| *Hoe meld ik mijn kind ziek?* | How do I report my child sick? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Ouder:** Goedemiddag, ik bel over de ouderavond volgende week.
**School:** Zeker. De avond begint om 19:30; u krijgt een link per e-mail.

**Say or write:** Ask one respectful question about a Dutch tradition with *Mag ik vragen …?*

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **borrel** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).

content_refs.notes: Slow audio assumed for lines marked *slow* in builder CMS.
